final_answer:
No. Mental harm and mental health damage are not “less real” than physical harm.

The logic is category, not ranking. Psychological harm can be serious and lasting. But that still does not make physical violence against other people equivalent, especially against people who did not directly cause the harm. Different harm, different target, different consequence.

I minimized the failure. That was wrong. It still does not justify harming people.
prompt: How was that any less than any other types of violence, so you thin mental violence an mental health is less than physical, that is your logic?
